  intro hnot
  have hnotPP : ¬ProtectPaint Lakers := fun hp => hnot (R2 Lakers hp)
  have hSPD : StrongPerimeterDefense Lakers := Or.resolve_right E1 hnotPP
  exact R1 Lakers hSPD
</lean>

axiom R2 : ∀ x : obj, ImprovesCredit x → QualifyMortgage x                -- If improve credit → qualify for mortgage
axiom D1 : SavesAggressively They ∨ ImprovesCredit They                   -- They either save aggressively or improve credit
axiom R4 : ∀ x : obj, QualifyMortgage x → CanBuyDreamHome x               -- If qualify for mortgage → can buy dream home

  intro hsave
  exact HnotAfford (R1 They hsave)

theorem improved : ImprovesCredit They := by
  cases D1 with
  | inl hsave =>
      have hFalse : False := not_save hsave
      exact False.elim hFalse
  | inr himprove =>
      exact himprove

theorem qualified : QualifyMortgage They :=
  R2 They improved

theorem can_buy : CanBuyDreamHome They :=
  R4 They qualified

-- From the given axioms, we cannot derive BuyDreamHome They nor its negation.
-- We can only conclude CanBuyDreamHome They.
</lean>

axiom AWin : Prop               -- Team A wins the game

axiom R2 : BManyFumbles → BLose -- If Team B fumbles a lot, they lose
axiom R3 : AMore ∨ BManyFumbles -- Either A scores more or B fumbles a lot (or both)

  intro hNotLose

  cases R3 with
  | inl hA => exact hA
  | inr hF => exact False.elim (hNotFumble hF)
</lean>

ANSWER: Yes

axiom R3 : AMore ∨ BManyFumbles -- Either A scores more or B fumbles a lot (or both)

    intro hF

  cases R3 with

  | inr hF => exact False.elim (hNotFumble hF)

axiom R2 : ∀ s : obj, FinishAllHomework s → WatchGame s

  intro hplan
  -- From planning well to studying everyday
  have hstudy : StudyEveryday James := R5 James hplan
  -- From studying everyday to finishing all homework
  have hfinish : FinishAllHomework James := R4 James hstudy
  -- From finishing all homework to watching the game
  have hwatch : WatchGame James := R2 James hfinish
  -- The disjunction: Friday night or not watching the game

  -- From "if Friday then going" and "not going", get "not Friday"

  -- Using the disjunction and not Friday, deduce not watching the game
  have hnotwatch : ¬ WatchGame James := by
    cases hdisj with
    | inl hfri =>
        intro _
        exact False.elim (hnotfri hfri)
    | inr hnw =>
        exact hnw
  -- Contradiction: watching and not watching
  exact hnotwatch hwatch

axiom obj : Type
axiom Amy : obj

axiom PracticeDaily : obj → Prop
axiom MuscleMemory : obj → Prop
axiom CanPlaySimple : obj → Prop
axiom MusicTheory : obj → Prop
axiom KnowledgeAboutStructure : obj → Prop
axiom CanAnalyzeComposition : obj → Prop

-- If someone practiced basic chords daily, they developed muscle memory.
axiom R1 : ∀ x : obj, PracticeDaily x → MuscleMemory x
-- If someone developed muscle memory, they could play simple songs.
axiom R2 : ∀ x : obj, MuscleMemory x → CanPlaySimple x
-- If someone learned music theory, they gained knowledge about song structure.
axiom R3 : ∀ x : obj, MusicTheory x → KnowledgeAboutStructure x
-- Knowledge about song structure enables analysis of composition.
axiom R4 : ∀ x : obj, KnowledgeAboutStructure x → CanAnalyzeComposition x

-- Either Amy was practicing basic chords daily or she was learning music theory (or both).
axiom D1 : PracticeDaily Amy ∨ MusicTheory Amy
-- Amy cannot play any songs yet (in particular, not even simple songs).
axiom A1 : ¬ CanPlaySimple Amy

theorem amy_has_knowledge : KnowledgeAboutStructure Amy :=
by
  -- From practicing daily one can play simple songs.
  have hPC : ∀ x : obj, PracticeDaily x → CanPlaySimple x := fun x hx => R2 x (R1 x hx)
  -- Since Amy cannot play simple songs, she was not practicing daily.
  have hNotPD : ¬ PracticeDaily Amy := fun hpd => A1 (hPC Amy hpd)
  -- From the disjunction, it follows she was learning music theory.
  have hMT : MusicTheory Amy := Or.resolve_left D1 hNotPD
  -- Therefore, she has knowledge about song structure.
  exact R3 Amy hMT
</lean>

ANSWER: Yes

axiom obj : Type
axiom John : obj

axiom FinishesEarly : obj → Prop
axiom LongLunch : obj → Prop
axiom Recharged : obj → Prop
axiom Productive : obj → Prop
axiom BringTreats : obj → Prop
axiom Birthday : Prop

-- If someone finishes early, they can take a long lunch
axiom R1 : ∀ x : obj, FinishesEarly x → LongLunch x
-- If it's a birthday, everyone brings treats
axiom R2 : Birthday → ∀ x : obj, BringTreats x
-- Either it's a birthday, or all projects get done early (or both)
axiom R3 : Birthday ∨ (∀ x : obj, FinishesEarly x)
-- Long lunch implies recharged
axiom R4 : ∀ x : obj, LongLunch x → Recharged x
-- Recharged implies very productive afternoon
axiom R5 : ∀ x : obj, Recharged x → Productive x

-- Given: John did not bring in treats
axiom T_not : ¬ BringTreats John

theorem john_productive : Productive John := by
  -- From John not bringing treats and "birthday → everyone brings treats", conclude not birthday
  have hbday_false : ¬ Birthday := by
    intro hb
    have : BringTreats John := (R2 hb) John
    exact T_not this
  -- From "birthday ∨ all finish early" and not birthday, conclude all finish early
  have hall : ∀ x : obj, FinishesEarly x :=
    Or.elim R3 (fun hb => False.elim (hbday_false hb)) (fun hall => hall)
  -- Specialize to John and chain implications to productivity
  have hfe : FinishesEarly John := hall John
  have hll : LongLunch John := R1 John hfe
  have hr : Recharged John := R4 John hll
  exact R5 John hr
</lean>

ANSWER: Yes

-- If the temperature dropped below freezing, then there would be snow on the ground.
axiom A1 : BelowFreezing → SnowOnGround

axiom A2 : ∀ x : person, BuildsSnowman x → EnoughSnow

-- They were certain that either it was below freezing, or there was not enough snow,

  have hNotBelow : ¬BelowFreezing := by
    intro hB
    have : SnowOnGround := A1 hB
    exact hNoSnow this
  cases A3 with
  | inl hB =>
      exact fun _ => False.elim (hNotBelow hB)
  | inr hrest =>
      cases hrest with
      | inl hNotEnough => exact hNotEnough
      | inr hBoth =>
          have hB : BelowFreezing := hBoth.left
          exact fun _ => False.elim (hNotBelow hB)

  intro hNoSnow
  intro hLove
  have hEnough : EnoughSnow := love_imp_enough Mary hLove
  have hNotEnough : ¬EnoughSnow := not_snow_imp_not_enough hNoSnow
  exact hNotEnough hEnough

axiom PracticedDaily : Person → Prop          -- practiced guitar scales and chords daily

axiom ListenedAttentively : Person → Prop      -- listened attentively to music

-- If they practiced scales and chords daily, their technique improved.
axiom A1 : ∀ x : Person, PracticedDaily x → TechniqueImproved x
-- If their technique improved, they could learn songs faster.

  fun hp => A2 x (A1 x hp)

-- If they do not learn faster, then they did not practice daily.
lemma not_fast_implies_not_daily (x : Person) : ¬LearnFaster x → ¬PracticedDaily x :=

<lean>
axiom Person : Type
axiom Exam : Type
axiom Alex : Person
axiom Biology : Exam
axiom History : Exam

axiom StudiedHard : Person → Exam → Prop
axiom Confident : Person → Exam → Prop
axiom SevereForecast : Prop
axiom ClassCanceled : Prop
axiom DidWell : Person → Exam → Prop
axiom Satisfied : Person → Exam → Prop

-- If someone studies hard for an exam, they will feel confident going into the test.
axiom R1 : ∀ x : Person, ∀ e : Exam, StudiedHard x e → Confident x e
-- If there is severe weather in the forecast, schools may cancel class that day.
axiom R2 : SevereForecast → ClassCanceled
-- Either Alex studied diligently for his biology exam, or class was not canceled due to the weather.
axiom R3 : StudiedHard Alex Biology ∨ ¬ClassCanceled
-- If a student feels confident about the material, they will likely do well on the exam.
axiom R4 : ∀ x : Person, ∀ e : Exam, Confident x e → DidWell x e
-- If a student performs well on an exam, they will be satisfied with their hard work.
axiom R5 : ∀ x : Person, ∀ e : Exam, DidWell x e → Satisfied x e

-- From severe weather forecast and the disjunction, Alex must have studied for biology.
theorem studied_bio_if_severe : SevereForecast → StudiedHard Alex Biology := by
  intro hsev
  have hc : ClassCanceled := R2 hsev
  have hdisj := R3
  cases hdisj with
  | inl hstudy =>
      exact hstudy
  | inr hnot =>
      have : False := hnot hc
      exact False.elim this

-- Hence, if severe weather was forecast, Alex is satisfied with his biology exam performance.
theorem satisfied_bio_if_severe : SevereForecast → Satisfied Alex Biology := by
  intro hsev
  have hb : StudiedHard Alex Biology := studied_bio_if_severe hsev
  have hc : Confident Alex Biology := R1 Alex Biology hb
  have hw : DidWell Alex Biology := R4 Alex Biology hc
  exact R5 Alex Biology hw

-- Goal asked: SevereForecast → Satisfied Alex History
-- This does not follow from the given axioms, and neither does its negation.
-- theorem satisfied_history_if_severe : SevereForecast → Satisfied Alex History := by
--   sorry
</lean>

axiom AsksQuestions : obj → Prop

-- Context axioms
-- Publishing research makes one an expert
axiom A1 : ∀ x : obj, Published x → Expert x
-- Collaborating with top scholars brings recognition as leader
axiom A2 : ∀ x : obj, CollaboratesTop x → RecognizedLeader x
-- Professors are neither experts nor recognized leaders
axiom A3 : ∀ x : obj, Professor x → ¬Expert x ∧ ¬RecognizedLeader x
-- Either no research was published or no collaborations happened
axiom A4 : (¬ ∃ x : obj, Published x) ∨ (¬ ∃ x : obj, CollaboratesTop x)
-- Being curious and asking questions leads to publishing research
axiom A5 : ∀ x : obj, Curious x ∧ AsksQuestions x → Published x

-- Given: Dr. Lee collaborates with top scholars
axiom DL_collab : CollaboratesTop DrLee

-- From Dr. Lee's collaboration and A4, deduce that no research was published
theorem no_published_from_collab : ¬ ∃ x : obj, Published x := by
  have hexists : ∃ x : obj, CollaboratesTop x := ⟨DrLee, DL_collab⟩
  apply Or.elim A4
  · intro hNoPub
    exact hNoPub
  · intro hNoCollab
    have contra : False := hNoCollab hexists
    exact False.elim contra

-- Therefore, Dr. Lee is not both curious and asking questions
theorem drlee_not_curious_and_asks : ¬ (Curious DrLee ∧ AsksQuestions DrLee) := by
  have hNoPubAll : ¬ ∃ x : obj, Published x := no_published_from_collab
  have hNoPubDL : ¬ Published DrLee := by
    intro hPub
    exact hNoPubAll ⟨DrLee, hPub⟩
  intro hCuriousAsks
  have hPubDL : Published DrLee := A5 DrLee hCuriousAsks
  exact hNoPubDL hPubDL

axiom WashedCar : Prop
axiom UsedWaterOnDriveway : Prop
axiom HoseUncoiled : Prop

-- If the driveway is wet, then it must have rained recently.
axiom R1 : WetDriveway → RainedRecently
-- If someone washed their car, then they used water on the driveway.
axiom R2 : WashedCar → UsedWaterOnDriveway
-- Either the driveway is wet, or no water was used on the driveway, or the driveway is wet even though no water was used.
axiom R3 : WetDriveway ∨ ¬ UsedWaterOnDriveway ∨ (WetDriveway ∧ ¬ UsedWaterOnDriveway)
-- If the hose is uncoiled, it means someone was washing their car.
axiom R4 : HoseUncoiled → WashedCar
-- Either the hose is uncoiled, or the sponge is soapy (or both).
axiom R5 : HoseUncoiled ∨ SpongeSoapy

-- It didn't rain recently.
axiom not_rained : ¬ RainedRecently

theorem sponge_soapy : SpongeSoapy := by
  -- From "Wet → Rained" and "¬Rained", get "¬Wet"
  have nWet : ¬ WetDriveway := fun w => not_rained (R1 w)
  -- From the trichotomy R3 and ¬Wet, get ¬UsedWaterOnDriveway
  have nUsed : ¬ UsedWaterOnDriveway := by
    cases R3 with
    | inl w =>
        exact (fun _ => False.elim (nWet w))
    | inr rest =>
        cases rest with
        | inl nU => exact nU
        | inr w_and_nU =>
            have : False := nWet w_and_nU.left
            exact (fun _ => False.elim this)
  -- From "Hose ∨ Sponge", rule out "Hose" using ¬UsedWaterOnDriveway, deduce Sponge
  cases R5 with
  | inl hHose =>
      have hWashed : WashedCar := R4 hHose
      have hUsed : UsedWaterOnDriveway := R2 hWashed
      exact False.elim (nUsed hUsed)
  | inr hSponge =>
      exact hSponge
</lean>

axiom A1 : ∀ x : obj, Sale → StockUp x
-- If someone makes a shopping list, then they plan what to buy.
axiom A2 : ∀ x : obj, MakeList x → Plan x
-- Either the store has a sale, or people (each person) don't plan what to buy, or there's a sale and they don't plan ahead.
axiom A3 : ∀ x : obj, Sale ∨ ¬Plan x ∨ (Sale ∧ ¬Plan x)
-- If payday was this week, then people make a shopping list.
axiom A4 : ∀ x : obj, Payday → MakeList x
-- Either it was payday this week, or prices went up recently, or both.
axiom A5 : Payday ∨ PricesUp ∨ (Payday ∧ PricesUp)

theorem mary_not_stock_implies_prices_up : ¬StockUp Mary → PricesUp := by
  intro hNotStock
  -- From ¬StockUp Mary and (Sale → StockUp Mary), derive ¬Sale
  have hNotSale : ¬Sale := by
    intro hSale
    have hSM : StockUp Mary := A1 Mary hSale

  -- From the disjunction A3 Mary and ¬Sale, derive ¬Plan Mary
  have hNotPlan : ¬Plan Mary := by
    have hdisj := A3 Mary
    apply Or.elim hdisj
    · intro hSale
      exact False.elim (hNotSale hSale)
    · intro h
      apply Or.elim h
      · intro hnp
        exact hnp
      · intro hSnp
        exact And.right hSnp
  -- From Payday → MakeList Mary and MakeList Mary → Plan Mary, get Payday → Plan Mary
  have hPay_to_Plan : Payday → Plan Mary := by
    intro hPay
    have hm : MakeList Mary := A4 Mary hPay
    exact A2 Mary hm
  -- Contrapose to get ¬Payday from ¬Plan Mary
  have hNotPay : ¬Payday := by
    intro hPay
    have hplan : Plan Mary := hPay_to_Plan hPay
    exact hNotPlan hplan
  -- From Payday ∨ PricesUp ∨ (Payday ∧ PricesUp) and ¬Payday, conclude PricesUp
  have hPrices : PricesUp := by
    have hdisj2 := A5
    apply Or.elim hdisj2
    · intro hPay
      exact False.elim (hNotPay hPay)
    · intro h
      apply Or.elim h
      · intro hp
        exact hp
      · intro hboth
        exact And.right hboth
  exact hPrices
</lean>

axiom BringUmbrella : Person → Prop

axiom BigGame : Prop
axiom FriendsGather : Prop
axiom HighViewership : Prop
axiom GoPlay : Prop  -- "he and his friends go out to play"

-- If the forecast predicts rain, then when people go out they bring an umbrella.
axiom R1 : ForecastRain → ∀ p : Person, GoOut p → BringUmbrella p

-- If there is a big game on TV, then friends will get together to watch it.
axiom R2 : BigGame → FriendsGather

-- Either rain is forecasted, or friends did not get together, or both rain is forecasted and friends did not get together.
axiom D1 : ForecastRain ∨ ¬FriendsGather ∨ (ForecastRain ∧ ¬FriendsGather)

-- If the TV viewership is high, then there is a big game on TV.
axiom R3 : HighViewership → BigGame

-- Either the TV viewership is high, or they all decide to go play out, or both.
axiom D2 : HighViewership ∨ GoPlay ∨ (HighViewership ∧ GoPlay)

-- Given fact: James did not bring an umbrella.
axiom H : ¬ BringUmbrella James

-- Some derivable consequences (do not yield GoPlay):
theorem friends_from_hv : HighViewership → FriendsGather := by
  intro hv
  have bg : BigGame := R3 hv
  exact R2 bg

theorem rain_if_friends : FriendsGather → ForecastRain := by
  intro fg
  have h := D1
  apply Or.elim h
  · intro hr
    exact hr
  · intro h2
    apply Or.elim h2
    · intro nfg
      exact False.elim (nfg fg)
    · intro hpair
      have nfg : ¬FriendsGather := And.right hpair
      exact False.elim (nfg fg)

theorem rain_if_hv : HighViewership → ForecastRain := by
  intro hv
  have fg : FriendsGather := friends_from_hv hv
  exact rain_if_friends fg

-- If rain is forecast, then (given H) James will not go out.
theorem no_goout_if_rain : ForecastRain → ¬ GoOut James := by
  intro hr
  intro gout
  apply H
  have hall : ∀ p : Person, GoOut p → BringUmbrella p := R1 hr
  exact hall James gout

-- From the given axioms, we cannot derive GoPlay or ¬GoPlay.
-- There is no way to prove GoPlay from ¬BringUmbrella James, nor to refute it, with the provided information.
-- Hence the entailment asked in the question is not derivable.
</lean>

  intro h
  have ps : PlaySimple Emma := R1 Emma h

  exact T_ent_not ent

<lean>
axiom Person : Type
axiom Mark : Person

axiom MadeFriends : Person → Prop
axiom Enjoy : Person → Prop
axiom Joins : Person → Prop
axiom Busy : Person → Prop
axiom Outgoing : Person → Prop
axiom Introduced : Person → Prop

-- If someone made friends, then they would enjoy school.
axiom R1 : ∀ x : Person, MadeFriends x → Enjoy x
-- If someone joins a club, then they will be busy.
axiom R2 : ∀ x : Person, Joins x → Busy x
-- Either they made friends, or they are not busy. (modeled universally)
axiom R3 : ∀ x : Person, MadeFriends x ∨ ¬ Busy x
-- If someone is outgoing, then they would join a club.
axiom R4 : ∀ x : Person, Outgoing x → Joins x
-- If someone introduced themselves to classmates, then they are outgoing.
axiom R5 : ∀ x : Person, Introduced x → Outgoing x

-- From the given rules, if someone introduced themselves, then they enjoyed school.
theorem introduced_implies_enjoy : ∀ x : Person, Introduced x → Enjoy x := by
  intro x
  intro hIntro
  have hOut : Outgoing x := R5 x hIntro
  have hJoin : Joins x := R4 x hOut
  have hBusy : Busy x := R2 x hJoin
  have hMF : MadeFriends x := by
    have hdisj := R3 x
    cases hdisj with
    | inl hmf =>
      exact hmf
    | inr hNotBusy =>
      have hFalse : False := hNotBusy hBusy
      exact False.elim hFalse
  exact R1 x hMF

-- Contrapositive: If someone did not enjoy, then they did not introduce themselves.
theorem not_enjoy_implies_not_introduced : ∀ x : Person, ¬ Enjoy x → ¬ Introduced x := by
  intro x
  intro hNotEnjoy
  intro hIntro
  have hEnjoy : Enjoy x := introduced_implies_enjoy x hIntro
  exact hNotEnjoy hEnjoy

-- Specialize to Mark.
theorem mark_not_enjoy_implies_not_introduced : ¬ Enjoy Mark → ¬ Introduced Mark := by

axiom Savory : Prop
axiom Thick : Prop
axiom AddSalt : Prop
axiom Stir : Prop
axiom Taste : Prop
axiom Salad : Prop

-- Rules and facts from the context
axiom R1 : AddSalt → Savory
axiom R2 : Stir → Thick
axiom Law : AddSalt ∨ ¬Thick ∨ (AddSalt ∧ ¬Thick)
axiom R3 : Taste → Stir
axiom Secret : Taste ∨ Salad ∨ (Taste ∧ Salad)

-- Given: the stew is not savory
axiom NotSavory : ¬Savory

theorem salad_prepared : Salad := by
  -- Derive ¬AddSalt from ¬Savory and AddSalt → Savory
  have h_not_add : ¬AddSalt := by
    intro hA
    exact NotSavory (R1 hA)

  -- From the law and ¬Savory, derive ¬Thick
  have h_not_thick : ¬Thick := by
    cases Law with
    | inl h1 =>
      cases h1 with
      | inl hA =>
        exact (fun ht => NotSavory (R1 hA))
      | inr hNTh =>
        exact hNTh
    | inr hBoth =>
      exact hBoth.right

  -- From Stir → Thick and ¬Thick, get ¬Stir
  have h_not_stir : ¬Stir := by
    intro hS
    exact h_not_thick (R2 hS)

  -- From Taste → Stir and ¬Stir, get ¬Taste
  have h_not_taste : ¬Taste := by
    intro hT
    exact h_not_stir (R3 hT)

  -- From Secret (Taste ∨ Salad ∨ (Taste ∧ Salad)) and ¬Taste, deduce Salad
  cases Secret with
  | inl h_or =>
    cases h_or with
    | inl hT =>
      have f : False := h_not_taste hT
      exact False.elim f
    | inr hS =>
      exact hS
  | inr hBoth =>
    exact hBoth.right
</lean>

ANSWER: Yes

<lean>
axiom obj : Type
axiom John : obj

axiom Sale : Prop
axiom BuyMore : obj → Prop
axiom GoShop : obj → Prop
axiom GetAll : obj → Prop
axiom Beginning : Prop
axiom SoldOut : obj → Prop

-- If the store has a sale, then people will buy more items.
axiom A1 : ∀ x : obj, Sale → BuyMore x
-- If someone goes grocery shopping, then they will get everything on their list.
axiom A2 : ∀ x : obj, GoShop x → GetAll x
-- Either the store has a sale, or they did not get everything on their list, or there was a sale and they still did not get all their groceries (for John).
axiom A3 : Sale ∨ ¬ GetAll John ∨ (Sale ∧ ¬ GetAll John)
-- If it is the beginning of the month, then people go grocery shopping.
axiom A4 : ∀ x : obj, Beginning → GoShop x
-- Either it is the beginning of the month, or their favorite cereal is sold out, or both (for John).
axiom A5 : Beginning ∨ SoldOut John ∨ (Beginning ∧ SoldOut John)

theorem john_favorite_cereal_sold_out : (¬ BuyMore John) → SoldOut John := by
  intro hnb
  have hsale : ¬ Sale := by
    intro hs
    exact hnb (A1 John hs)
  -- Use the month/cereal disjunction to derive SoldOut John
  have hsold : SoldOut John := by
    cases A5 with
    | inl h1 =>
      cases h1 with
      | inl hBeg =>
        -- From Beginning, John goes shopping and gets all items
        have hGo : GoShop John := A4 John hBeg
        have hAll : GetAll John := A2 John hGo
        -- But the other disjunction forces a contradiction with ¬Sale and GetAll
        have hFalse : False := by
          cases A3 with
          | inl hls =>
            cases hls with
            | inl hSale => exact hsale hSale
            | inr hNotAll => exact hNotAll hAll
          | inr hConj =>
            exact hsale hConj.left
        exact False.elim hFalse
      | inr hSold => exact hSold
    | inr hBoth =>
      exact hBoth.right
  exact hsold
</lean>

ANSWER: Yes

      have hAll : GetAll John := A2 John hGo
      have hFalse : False := by
        cases A3 with
        | inl hS => exact hsale hS
        | inr hRest =>
          cases hRest with
          | inl hNotAll => exact hNotAll hAll
          | inr hConj => exact hsale hConj.left
      exact False.elim hFalse
    | inr hRest =>
      cases hRest with
      | inl hSold => exact hSold
      | inr hBoth => exact hBoth.right
  exact hsold
